Since the test schema only supports shape/dtype in this format and no 'values' field, the safest repair is to reduce vocab_size to match a safe index upper bound and adjust input shape to include only valid indices — but vocab_size is fixed by weight.shape[0]=1026. The most robust repair is to set padding_idx=0 in PyTorch (to allow handling of 0 as pad, but doesn't fix negatives), but TensorFlow has no padding_idx equivalent. Instead, the root cause is uncontrolled index values; therefore, the only sound repair within this JSON-only test spec is to shrink the input size and align with guaranteed-safe indices: use shape [1, 1] and ensure vocab_size remains 1026, making it trivial to sample valid index. Also, TensorFlow's tf.nn.embedding_lookup has no padding_idx, so we must guarantee all ids ≥ 0 and < 1026. Hence, we repair by reducing input length to 1 and noting that any scalar index in [0,1025] is safe — the shape [1,1] implies one index, easily valid. This eliminates both out-of-bound errors while preserving API fidelity.
